0.content:
About the DPA v1.2.0. It's:
 - Safe: The Dashboards app and the DTR are both restricted to read-only permissions/calls by design. No data ever leaves the Dynatrace tenant.
 - Fast: The analytics happens where the data resides, in Grail.
 - Open source: Copy, inspect, customize and share it in a few clicks.

1.content:
-DTR version: 1.2.0
- [Download latest version (public GitHub)](https://github.com/dynatrace-oss/CustomerSuccess)


Changelog:
- Fixed 3rd Gen Adoption Rate: now measures actual user adoption (users on Gen3 vs Classic via audit logs) instead of event proportion
- All DQL queries inherit dashboard timeframe selector (no hardcoded timeframes on outer fetches)
- Cross-references CLASSIC_API audit events for accurate Gen2 user identification

activity-heatmap.description: Platform activity distribution across days of the week (30-day window). Identifies peak usage days and weekend drop-off patterns. Format: e=numeric day, E=day name.
activity-heatmap.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| fieldsAdd dow = concat(formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"e"), "-", formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"E"))
| summarize activity = count(), by:{dow}
| sort dow asc
activity-heatmap.title: Activity by Day of Week

adoption-maturity.description: Composite score 0-100 across four dimensions: Users (25pts if >=10 unique), Apps (25pts if >=5 used), API (25pts if >100 calls), AI (25pts if >50 davis calls). Tracks overall platform maturity.
adoption-maturity.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT" AND event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| summarize uniqueUsers = countDistinct(`user.id`), totalApps = countDistinct(`dt.app.id`), apiCalls = countIf(`request.source` == "API"), aiCalls = countIf(contains(toString(resource), "/davis/"))
| fieldsAdd userPts = if(uniqueUsers >= 10, then:25, else:uniqueUsers * 2 + 5)
| fieldsAdd appPts = if(totalApps >= 5, then:25, else:totalApps * 5)
| fieldsAdd apiPts = if(apiCalls > 100, then:25, else:if(apiCalls > 0, then:15, else:0))
| fieldsAdd aiPts = if(aiCalls > 50, then:25, else:if(aiCalls > 0, then:12, else:0))
| fieldsAdd maturityScore = userPts + appPts + apiPts + aiPts
| fields maturityScore
adoption-maturity.title: Maturity Score

ai-agent-activity.description: AI-related API calls from external agents, MCP tools, and integrations. Identifies which AI endpoints are consumed programmatically and by whom.
ai-agent-activity.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "API"
| filter contains(resource, "/davis/") OR contains(resource, "/ai/") OR contains(resource, "/copilot/")
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize calls = count(), uniqueCallers = countDistinct(userEmail), by:{resource}
| sort calls desc
| limit 15
ai-agent-activity.title: External AI/Agent Access

ai-davis-analyzers.description: Daily Davis Analyzer invocation volume. Analyzers provide AI-powered root cause analysis and anomaly detection. Growing usage indicates maturing AIOps adoption.
ai-davis-analyzers.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter contains(resource, "/davis/analyzer")
| makeTimeseries analyzerCalls = count()
ai-davis-analyzers.title: Davis Analyzer Trend

ai-usage-per-user.description: Per-user breakdown of AI feature usage showing total AI calls, Copilot interactions, and Analyzer invocations. Identifies AI champions and adoption gaps.
ai-usage-per-user.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter contains(resource, "/davis/")
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize aiCalls = count(), copilotCalls = countIf(contains(resource, "/copilot/")), analyzerCalls = countIf(contains(resource, "/analyzer")), by:{userEmail}
| sort aiCalls desc
| limit 15
ai-usage-per-user.title: AI Usage per User

apps-2g-migration-candidates.title: Migration Candidates

apps-2g-usage.title: Legacy Feature Usage

apps-3g-ranking.description: Ranked list of 3rd-generation apps by total page views, unique users, and sessions. Identifies the most-adopted features and underutilized capabilities.
apps-3g-ranking.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter isNotNull(`dt.app.id`)
| filter `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.appshell" AND `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.launcher"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize pageViews = count(), uniqueUsers = countDistinct(userEmail), sessions = countDistinct(`origin.session`), by:{`dt.app.id`}
| sort pageViews desc
apps-3g-ranking.title: App Ranking

apps-3g-trend.description: Daily stacked area of app visits over time. Rising trends indicate growing adoption; declining apps may need enablement or have been superseded.
apps-3g-trend.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter isNotNull(`dt.app.id`)
| filter `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.appshell" AND `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.launcher"
| makeTimeseries visits = count(), by:{`dt.app.id`}
apps-3g-trend.title: App Usage Trend

apps-3g-users-per-app.description: Daily unique users per app. Identifies which apps have growing audiences versus shrinking ones, independent of total click volume.
apps-3g-users-per-app.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter isNotNull(`dt.app.id`)
| filter `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.appshell" AND `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.launcher"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| makeTimeseries uniqueUsers = countDistinct(userEmail), by:{`dt.app.id`}
apps-3g-users-per-app.title: Users per App (Trend)

classic-vs-platform-api.description: Daily trend of Platform API (API_GATEWAY) vs Classic API calls. A declining classic line indicates successful modernization. Target is full platform API adoption.
classic-vs-platform-api.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY" OR event.provider == "CLASSIC_API"
| makeTimeseries platform = countIf(event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"), classic = countIf(event.provider == "CLASSIC_API")
classic-vs-platform-api.title: Platform vs Classic API Migration

daily-activity-trend.description: Daily event volume split by source. BROWSER = human users, DT_SERVERLESS = workflows/automation, OTHER = API tokens and integrations.
daily-activity-trend.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| makeTimeseries total = count(), by:{`request.source`}
daily-activity-trend.title: Daily Activity by Source

daily-returning-users.description: Users active on 2+ distinct days in the last 7 days. Always looks at a fixed 7-day window regardless of dashboard timeframe.
daily-returning-users.query:
fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-7d
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-7d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| fieldsAdd day = formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"yyyy-MM-dd")
| summarize activeDays = countDistinct(day), by:{userEmail}
| filter activeDays >= 2
| summarize returningUsers = count()
daily-returning-users.title: Returning Users

daily-unique-users.description: Daily distinct active users over time. The primary adoption trend indicator. Sustained growth signals expanding platform value; drops warrant investigation.
daily-unique-users.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| makeTimeseries uniqueUsers = countDistinct(userEmail)
daily-unique-users.title: Daily Active Users

davis-adoption-report.description: Davis Copilot-generated adoption report. Covers total users/sessions with MoM delta, Classic vs 3rd Gen split, top trending and fading apps, top 5 user scorecard, and actionable next steps. Source: AUDIT_EVENT, last 30 days vs prior 30 days.
davis-adoption-report.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| lookup [
    fetch dt.system.events
    | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
    | filter contains(toString(`user.email`),"@")
    | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`),"@heimdall")
    | summarize email=takeFirst(`user.email`), by:{`user.id`}
  ], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| fieldsAdd platform = if(isNotNull(`dt.app.id`) AND matchesPhrase(`dt.app.id`,"classic",caseSensitive:false),"Classic",else:"3rd Gen")
| summarize classic=countIf(platform=="Classic"), new=countIf(platform=="3rd Gen"), by:{`origin.session`, userEmail}
| fieldsAdd platform = if(classic>new,"Classic",else:"3rd Gen")
| summarize classic=countIf(platform=="Classic"), new=countIf(platform=="3rd Gen"), sessions=countDistinct(`origin.session`), by:{userEmail}
| fieldsAdd platform = if(classic>new,"Classic",else:"3rd Gen")
| summarize users=countDistinct(userEmail), sessions=sum(sessions), classic_sessions=sum(classic), new_sessions=sum(new), classic_user=countIf(platform=="Classic"), new_user=countIf(platform=="3rd Gen")
| fieldsAdd join=1
| join on:{join}, prefix:{"trend."}, [
    fetch dt.system.events
    | filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
    | filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
    | filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
    | filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
    | lookup [
        fetch dt.system.events
        | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
        | filter contains(toString(`user.email`),"@")
        | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`),"@heimdall")
        | summarize email=takeFirst(`user.email`), by:{`user.id`}
      ], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
    | fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
    | fieldsAdd platform = if(isNotNull(`dt.app.id`) AND matchesPhrase(`dt.app.id`,"classic",caseSensitive:false),"Classic",else:"3rd Gen")
    | summarize classic=countIf(platform=="Classic"), new=countIf(platform=="3rd Gen"), by:{`origin.session`, userEmail}
    | fieldsAdd platform = if(classic>new,"Classic",else:"3rd Gen")
    | summarize classic=countIf(platform=="Classic"), new=countIf(platform=="3rd Gen"), sessions=countDistinct(`origin.session`), by:{userEmail}
    | fieldsAdd platform = if(classic>new,"Classic",else:"3rd Gen")
    | summarize users=countDistinct(userEmail), sessions=sum(sessions), classic_sessions=sum(classic), new_sessions=sum(new), classic_user=countIf(platform=="Classic"), new_user=countIf(platform=="3rd Gen")
    | fieldsAdd join=1
]
| fieldsAdd MoM_users=round(((toDouble(users)-toDouble(trend.users))/toDouble(trend.users))*100, decimals:1),
            MoM_classic_users=round(((toDouble(classic_user)-toDouble(trend.classic_user))/toDouble(trend.classic_user))*100, decimals:1),
            MoM_new_users=round(((toDouble(new_user)-toDouble(trend.new_user))/toDouble(trend.new_user))*100, decimals:1)
| fieldsAdd MoM_sessions=round(((toDouble(sessions)-toDouble(trend.sessions))/toDouble(trend.sessions))*100, decimals:1),
            MoM_classic_sessions=round(((toDouble(classic_sessions)-toDouble(trend.classic_sessions))/toDouble(trend.classic_sessions))*100, decimals:1),
            MoM_new_sessions=round(((toDouble(new_sessions)-toDouble(trend.new_sessions))/toDouble(trend.new_sessions))*100, decimals:1)
| fieldsRemove trend.join
| join on:{join}, prefix:{"app_adoption."}, [
    fetch dt.system.events
    | filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
    | filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
    | filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
    | filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
    | filter isNotNull(`dt.app.id`) AND `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.appshell" AND `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.launcher"
    | lookup [
        fetch dt.system.events
        | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
        | filter contains(toString(`user.email`),"@")
        | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`),"@heimdall")
        | summarize email=takeFirst(`user.email`), by:{`user.id`}
      ], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
    | fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
    | fieldsAdd platform = if(matchesPhrase(`dt.app.id`,"classic",caseSensitive:false),"Classic",else:"3rd Gen")
    | summarize sessions=countDistinct(`origin.session`), users=countDistinct(userEmail), latest_activity=formatTimestamp(takeFirst(timestamp),format:"MM-dd-yyyy"), by:{app=`dt.app.name`, platform}
    | join on:{app, platform}, prefix:"trend.", [
        fetch dt.system.events
        | filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
        | filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
        | filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
        | filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
        | filter isNotNull(`dt.app.id`) AND `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.appshell" AND `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.launcher"
        | lookup [
            fetch dt.system.events
            | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
            | filter contains(toString(`user.email`),"@")
            | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`),"@heimdall")
            | summarize email=takeFirst(`user.email`), by:{`user.id`}
          ], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
        | fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
        | fieldsAdd platform = if(matchesPhrase(`dt.app.id`,"classic",caseSensitive:false),"Classic",else:"3rd Gen")
        | summarize sessions=countDistinct(`origin.session`), users=countDistinct(userEmail), latest_activity=formatTimestamp(takeFirst(timestamp),format:"MM-dd-yyyy"), by:{app=`dt.app.name`, platform}
    ]
    | fieldsAdd MoM_users=round(((toDouble(users)-toDouble(trend.users))/toDouble(trend.users))*100, decimals:1),
                MoM_sessions=round(((toDouble(sessions)-toDouble(trend.sessions))/toDouble(trend.sessions))*100, decimals:1)
    | fieldsRemove trend.platform, trend.app
    | summarize data=collectArray(record(app, platform, MoM_users, MoM_sessions, users, sessions, latest_activity, trend.users, trend.sessions))
    | fieldsAdd join=1
]
| join on:{join}, prefix:{"top_users."}, [
    fetch dt.system.events
    | filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
    | filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
    | filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
    | filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
    | lookup [
        fetch dt.system.events
        | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
        | filter contains(toString(`user.email`),"@")
        | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`),"@heimdall")
        | summarize email=takeFirst(`user.email`), by:{`user.id`}
      ], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
    | fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
    | fieldsAdd platform = if(isNotNull(`dt.app.id`) AND matchesPhrase(`dt.app.id`,"classic",caseSensitive:false),"Classic",else:"3rd Gen")
    | summarize sessions=countDistinct(`origin.session`), classic_count=countIf(platform=="Classic"), new_count=countIf(platform=="3rd Gen"), total=count(), last_activity=formatTimestamp(takeFirst(timestamp),format:"MM-dd-yyyy"), by:{userEmail}
    | fieldsAdd classic_pct=round(toDouble(classic_count)/toDouble(total)*100, decimals:0), platform_pct=round(toDouble(new_count)/toDouble(total)*100, decimals:0)
    | sort sessions desc
    | limit 5
    | summarize data=collectArray(record(user=userEmail, sessions, percent_on_classic=classic_pct, percent_on_platform=platform_pct, last_activity_date=last_activity))
    | fieldsAdd join=1
]
| fields users, sessions, classic_sessions, new_sessions, classic_user, new_user,
         MoM_users, MoM_sessions, MoM_classic_users, MoM_new_users, MoM_classic_sessions, MoM_new_sessions,
         trend.users, trend.sessions,
         app_adoption=app_adoption.data, top_users=top_users.data, join
| fieldsAdd
    prompt=concat("Analyze the Dynatrace Platform Adoption data provided and generate a comprehensive Dynatrace Admin adoption report with Classic vs 3rd Gen migration insights, top app trends, user engagement analysis, risk assessment, and actionable next steps."),
    execute=true,
    contexts=array(
      record(
        type="supplementary",
        value=toString(record(users, sessions, classic_sessions, new_sessions, classic_user, new_user, MoM_users, MoM_sessions, MoM_classic_users, MoM_new_users, MoM_classic_sessions, MoM_new_sessions, trend.users, trend.sessions, app_adoption, top_users))
      ),
      record(
        type="instruction",
        value="RULES: Reference ONLY the data. Never hallucinate. If a value is 0 say No activity recorded. OUTPUT clean Markdown with ALL sections: (1) Executive Summary 200 words with health verdict Healthy or At Risk or Critical, adoption momentum, Classic vs 3rd Gen share with MoM, sessions per user ratio, biggest risk or opportunity. (2) Platform Health Scorecard as table with columns Metric, Current, Previous Period, MoM Change percent, Status. Rows for Total Users, Total Sessions, Avg Sessions per User, Classic Users, 3rd Gen Users, Classic Sessions, 3rd Gen Sessions. Status use green for growing or stable, yellow for declining 1-20 percent, red for declining over 20 percent or zero. (3) Migration Progress with Adoption Rate percent, Session Share percent, Migration Velocity accelerating or stable or decelerating, Remaining Classic Gap, Risk Assessment. (4) App Adoption Deep Dive with 3rd Gen Apps table from app_adoption data sorted by sessions descending with columns App Name, Users, Sessions, MoM Users percent, MoM Sessions percent, Trend. Identify top 3 growing, top 3 declining, any abandoned apps. Then Classic Apps same table or state full migration achieved if none. (5) User Engagement Analysis with Top Users table from top_users data with columns User, Sessions, Percent Classic, Percent 3rd Gen, Last Active, Risk Level where Green means over 80 percent 3rd Gen, Yellow 50-80, Red under 50. Then Engagement Segments counting Power Users over 20 sessions listing names, Regular 5-20 count, Light under 5 as churn risk. (6) Risk Register ranked High Medium Low covering Classic dependency, engagement decline, feature abandonment, concentration risk with evidence and mitigation for each. (7) Recommended Actions prioritized P1 P2 P3 naming specific Classic-heavy users for migration, enablement gaps, growth apps to promote, quick wins, relevant Dynatrace documentation links. (8) 30-Day Forecast projecting next month user count, time to full Classic deprecation if Classic users exist, recommended targets for growth and adoption rate."
      )
    )
davis-adoption-report.title: Platform Adoption Report (Davis AI)

davis-copilot-usage.description: Daily Davis Copilot interaction volume by access source. Browser = UI copilot chat, DT_SERVERLESS = workflow-triggered, API = external integrations and MCP tools.
davis-copilot-usage.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter contains(resource, "/davis/copilot/")
| makeTimeseries davisUsage = count(), by:{`request.source`}
davis-copilot-usage.title: Davis Copilot Usage

dormant-users.description: Users active in the last 30 days but not seen in the last 14 days. Fixed 30-day window ΓÇö always identifies churn risk regardless of dashboard timeframe.
dormant-users.query:
fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT" AND event.provider == "API_GATEWAY" AND `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize lastSeen = max(timestamp), by:{userEmail}
| filter lastSeen < now()-14d
| summarize dormantUsers = count()
dormant-users.title: Dormant Users

dql-query-volume.description: Daily DQL query executions by real users (excludes system and service accounts). Growing query volume indicates increasing Grail adoption and self-service analytics maturity.
dql-query-volume.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
| filter NOT contains(toString(user.email), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com")
| filter NOT contains(toString(user.email), "@service.sso.dynatrace.com")
| filter contains(toString(user.email), "@")
| makeTimeseries queries = count()
dql-query-volume.title: DQL Query Volume

feature-treemap.description: Visual map of feature usage sized by interaction volume. Larger blocks represent more heavily used apps. Excludes appshell and launcher navigation noise.
feature-treemap.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter isNotNull(`dt.app.id`)
| filter `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.appshell" AND `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.launcher"
| summarize usage = count(), uniqueUsers = countDistinct(`user.id`), by:{`dt.app.id`}
| sort usage desc
| limit 25
feature-treemap.title: Feature Usage Map

gen3-adoption-detail.title: Gen3 Adoption Breakdown

gen3-adoption-pct.title: Gen3 Adoption Rate

header.content:
## Dynatrace Platform Adoption Dashboard
Real-time adoption analytics powered by Grail audit telemetry. All metrics exclude system accounts and internal services.

human-vs-automation.description: Daily activity split: BROWSER (human interactive), DT_SERVERLESS (workflows and automation), OTHER (API tokens and integrations). Growing automation ratio indicates platform maturity.
human-vs-automation.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| makeTimeseries browser = countIf(`request.source` == "BROWSER"), serverless = countIf(`request.source` == "DT_SERVERLESS"), other = countIf(`request.source` == "OTHER")
human-vs-automation.title: Human vs Automation

kpi-session-depth.description: Average number of API calls per browser session. Higher depth indicates users are exploring more features per visit rather than single-action check-ins.
kpi-session-depth.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT" AND event.provider == "API_GATEWAY" AND `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| filter isNotNull(`origin.session`)
| summarize actions = count(), by:{`origin.session`}
| summarize avgDepth = round(avg(toDouble(actions)), decimals:1)
kpi-session-depth.title: Session Depth

kpi-stickiness.description: DAU/MAU ratio: average active days per user divided by 30, expressed as percentage. Higher value indicates stickier platform usage. Industry benchmark: 20-30% is good, 50%+ is exceptional.
kpi-stickiness.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT" AND event.provider == "API_GATEWAY" AND `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| fieldsAdd day = formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"yyyy-MM-dd")
| summarize activeDays = countDistinct(day), by:{userEmail}
| summarize stickiness = round(100.0 * avg(toDouble(activeDays)) / 30.0, decimals:1)
kpi-stickiness.title: Stickiness (DAU/MAU)

kpi-total-sessions.description: Total distinct browser sessions in the selected timeframe. A session is identified by the origin.session field from the audit trail.
kpi-total-sessions.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| summarize totalSessions = countDistinct(`origin.session`)
kpi-total-sessions.title: Total Sessions

kpi-unique-users.description: Distinct human users (resolved to email) who performed any browser action in the selected timeframe. Excludes system accounts and service principals.
kpi-unique-users.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@service.sso.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| filter isNotNull(`lu.email`)
| summarize uniqueUsers = countDistinct(`lu.email`)
kpi-unique-users.title: Active Users

lifecycle-cohorts.description: User lifecycle segmentation: New (first seen within 7 days), Active (veteran users active in last 3 days), At Risk (last seen 7-14 days ago), Churned (last seen 14+ days ago).
lifecycle-cohorts.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT" AND event.provider == "API_GATEWAY" AND `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| summarize firstSeen = min(timestamp), lastSeen = max(timestamp), by:{user.id}
| fieldsAdd daysSinceFirstSeen = toDouble(now() - firstSeen) / 86400000000000.0
| fieldsAdd daysSinceLastSeen = toDouble(now() - lastSeen) / 86400000000000.0
| fieldsAdd cohort = if(daysSinceFirstSeen <= 7, then:"1-New", else:if(daysSinceLastSeen <= 3, then:"2-Active", else:if(daysSinceLastSeen <= 14, then:"3-At Risk", else:"4-Churned")))
| summarize users = count(), by:{cohort}
| sort cohort asc
lifecycle-cohorts.title: User Lifecycle Cohorts

observability-domain-coverage.description: Daily DQL queries split by target data table. A well-adopted platform queries across multiple domains (logs, metrics, spans, events, entities). Narrow coverage signals missed opportunities.
observability-domain-coverage.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com")
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@service.sso.dynatrace.com")
| filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@")
| filter isNotNull(`table`) AND `table` != ""
| makeTimeseries queries = count(), by:{`table`}
observability-domain-coverage.title: Domain Coverage

peak-login-hours.description: Login event distribution by hour (UTC). Reveals which timezones have the most active users. Peak hours indicate primary team locations and optimal maintenance windows.
peak-login-hours.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter event.type == "LOGIN"
| fieldsAdd hour = formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"HH")
| summarize logins = count(), by:{hour}
| sort hour asc
peak-login-hours.title: Login Distribution by Hour (UTC)

platform-breadth.description: Distribution of platform breadth: how many distinct apps each user touches. Users in 7+ apps are power users leveraging the full platform. Single-app users may need enablement.
platform-breadth.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT" AND event.provider == "API_GATEWAY" AND `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| summarize appCount = countDistinct(`dt.app.id`), by:{user.id}
| fieldsAdd segment = if(appCount == 1, then:"1 App", else:if(appCount <= 3, then:"2-3 Apps", else:if(appCount <= 6, then:"4-6 Apps", else:"7+ Apps")))
| summarize users = count(), by:{segment}
| sort segment asc
platform-breadth.title: Platform Breadth

platform-cost.description: DPS billing consumption by category. Shows which capabilities consume the most platform units. Use to identify cost drivers and optimization opportunities.
platform-cost.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "BILLING_USAGE_EVENT"
| summarize volume = sum(toDouble(`billed_quantity`)), records = count(), by:{event.type}
| sort volume desc
| limit 10
platform-cost.title: Platform Cost by Category

query-analytics.description: DQL query activity broken down by target data table. Shows which Grail tables are most queried, average execution duration, and total data scanned. Gaps reveal underutilized data domains.
query-analytics.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com")
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@service.sso.dynatrace.com")
| filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@")
| summarize queries = count(), uniqueUsers = countDistinct(`user.email`), avgDurationMs = round(avg(toDouble(`execution_duration_ms`)), decimals:1), scannedGB = round(sum(toDouble(`scanned_bytes`)) / 1073741824, decimals:3), by:{`table`}
| sort queries desc
query-analytics.title: Query Activity by Table

read-write-ratio.description: Read (GET) vs Write (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) ratio per app. High write ratio indicates active content creation; low write ratio suggests passive consumption. Helps identify power-use patterns.
read-write-ratio.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT" AND event.provider == "API_GATEWAY" AND `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter isNotNull(`dt.app.id`) AND `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.appshell" AND `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.launcher"
| fieldsAdd readAction = if(event.type == "GET", then:1, else:0)
| fieldsAdd writeAction = if(event.type == "POST" OR event.type == "PUT" OR event.type == "PATCH" OR event.type == "DELETE", then:1, else:0)
| summarize reads = sum(readAction), writes = sum(writeAction), by:{`dt.app.id`}
| fieldsAdd total = reads + writes
| fieldsAdd writeRatioPct = round(100.0 * toDouble(writes) / toDouble(total), decimals:1)
| sort total desc
| limit 15
| fields `dt.app.id`, reads, writes, total, writeRatioPct
read-write-ratio.title: Read vs Write by App

top-users.description: Top 20 most active users by total browser actions with active days, sessions, and apps touched. Use to identify platform champions and validate engagement breadth.
top-users.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize totalActions = count(), activeDays = countDistinct(formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"yyyy-MM-dd")), sessions = countDistinct(`origin.session`), appsUsed = countDistinct(`dt.app.id`), by:{userEmail}
| sort totalActions desc
| limit 20
top-users.title: Top 20 Power Users

user-engagement-scorecard.description: Comprehensive per-user engagement scorecard showing active days, sessions, browser actions, apps used, DQL queries executed, tables queried, and data scanned. Sorted by total activity.
user-engagement-scorecard.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize browserActions = count(), sessions = countDistinct(`origin.session`), activeDays = countDistinct(formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"yyyy-MM-dd")), appsUsed = countDistinct(`dt.app.id`), by:{userEmail}
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize queries = count(), tablesQueried = countDistinct(`table`), scannedGB = sum(toDouble(`scanned_bytes`)) / 1073741824, by: {`user.email`}], sourceField:userEmail, lookupField:`user.email`, prefix:"q."
| fieldsAdd queries = toLong(`q.queries`), tablesQueried = toLong(`q.tablesQueried`), scannedGB = round(toDouble(`q.scannedGB`), decimals:2)
| fields userEmail, activeDays, sessions, browserActions, appsUsed, queries, tablesQueried, scannedGB
| sort browserActions desc
| limit 100
user-engagement-scorecard.title: User Engagement Scorecard

weekly-growth-trend.description: Week-by-week growth in unique users and total actions. Consistent growth across both metrics indicates healthy organic adoption rather than automated noise.
weekly-growth-trend.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT" AND event.provider == "API_GATEWAY" AND `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| fieldsAdd week = concat("W", formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"w"))
| summarize users = countDistinct(user.id), actions = count(), by:{week}
| sort week asc
weekly-growth-trend.title: Weekly Growth

weekly-returning-users.description: Users active in 2+ distinct ISO weeks in the last 14 days. Fixed 14-day window ΓÇö always shows weekly retention regardless of dashboard timeframe.
weekly-returning-users.query:
fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-14d
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-14d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| fieldsAdd week = formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"yyyy-'W'ww")
| summarize activeWeeks = countDistinct(week), by:{userEmail}
| filter activeWeeks >= 2
| summarize weeklyReturning = count()
weekly-returning-users.title: Weekly Returners

workflow-health.description: Workflow success rates by workflow name. Color-coded: green >=90%, yellow <90%, red <50%. Low success rates indicate broken automations requiring immediate attention.
workflow-health.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "WORKFLOW_EVENT"
| filter event.type == "ACTION_EXECUTION"
| filter `dt.automation_engine.state.is_final` == true
| summarize executions = count(), successes = countIf(`dt.automation_engine.state` == "SUCCESS"), failures = countIf(`dt.automation_engine.state` != "SUCCESS"), by:{`dt.automation_engine.workflow.title`}
| fieldsAdd successRate = round(100.0 * toDouble(successes) / toDouble(executions), decimals:1)
| sort executions desc
| limit 15
workflow-health.title: Workflow Health

workflow-trend.description: Daily workflow action executions split by final state. Growing SUCCESS volume with flat FAILED indicates healthy automation scaling.
workflow-trend.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "WORKFLOW_EVENT"
| filter event.type == "ACTION_EXECUTION"
| filter `dt.automation_engine.state.is_final` == true
| makeTimeseries total = count(), by:{`dt.automation_engine.state`}
workflow-trend.title: Workflow Execution Trend

wow-summary.description: Side-by-side comparison of the current 7-day window versus the prior 7 days. Tracks users, sessions, total actions, and engagement depth to identify momentum shifts.
wow-summary.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT" AND event.provider == "API_GATEWAY" AND `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" AND `user.id` != "system"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| fieldsAdd period = if(timestamp >= now()-7d, then:"This Week", else:"Prior Week")
| summarize users = countDistinct(userEmail), sessions = countDistinct(`origin.session`), actions = count(), by:{period}
| fieldsAdd actionsPerSession = round(toDouble(actions) / toDouble(sessions), decimals:1)
| fields period, users, sessions, actions, actionsPerSession
| sort period desc
wow-summary.title: Week-over-Week Comparison
